meta_title: For Founders: analytics setup to Solve can't finish projects (Advanced Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome can't finish projects. A Advanced-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Analytics Setup to Overcome Project Stagnation
content.seo_description: Stop abandoning projects. Learn how a robust analytics setup using Antigravity and Claude Code provides the data-driven clarity needed to ship your MVP.
content.answer_block: Most founders abandon projects because they lack objective feedback loops. By integrating high-fidelity analytics, you transform 'gut feeling' into actionable data. This setup shifts your focus from endless feature creep to user-validated revenue drivers, ensuring your MVP remains lean, focused, and ultimately, profitable.
content.intro:
The graveyard of SaaS is littered with 'perfect' code that never saw a single paying user. You’ve been there: three months into development, the initial fire fades, the feature list grows, and the lack of concrete user data leaves you paralyzed. You aren't failing because you can't code; you're failing because you're flying blind without a telemetry suite to tell you what actually moves the needle.

True mastery isn't just shipping; it's measuring what you ship. By embedding professional-grade analytics into your architecture from day one, you strip away the ambiguity. We are moving beyond simple page views and into event-driven behavioral tracking that forces you to confront the reality of your product-market fit, effectively killing the paralysis that keeps your best ideas in the repository.

steps.title: Define Your North Star Metric
steps.description: Before instrumenting, identify the single event that defines user success. Without this, your analytics will be noise.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current SaaS codebase and suggest a minimal set of 5 core events (e.g., user_signup, feature_click) that map directly to revenue generation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'identify the core conversion events for my MVP based on the current user flow'

steps.title: Architecting the Telemetry Layer
steps.description: Create a decoupled analytics service to prevent vendor lock-in and ensure your business logic remains clean.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, modular TypeScript analytics wrapper that abstracts event tracking so I can swap providers without changing business logic.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create a new src/services/analytics.ts file with a singleton pattern for event tracking'

steps.title: Instrumenting High-Value Actions
steps.description: Inject tracking into critical path components like signup, checkout, and core feature utilization.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review my existing auth and checkout components and inject the analytics wrapper calls to track success and failure rates.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'apply tracking to the auth/signup and checkout/payment components'

steps.title: Implementing Real-time Dashboards
steps.description: Bridge the gap between raw events and visual insights using a lightweight dashboard provider.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a configuration script to sync my event schema with a dashboard tool, ensuring all properties are typed and consistent.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate the sync configuration for my analytics provider and validate the event schema'

steps.title: Closing the Feedback Loop
steps.description: Set up automated alerts for drop-off points to identify exactly where users are quitting.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create an automated alert logic that notifies me via email or Slack when the conversion rate on the signup funnel drops below 20%.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'implement a monitoring hook that alerts if funnel conversion rates drop below the defined threshold'

pro_tips: Track 'Intent' rather than just 'Action'; monitor clicks on buttons that don't exist yet to validate demand before building features.
Use distinct user IDs for anonymous sessions to bridge the gap between pre-signup behavior and post-signup conversion.
Keep your event payload lean; sending excessive metadata increases latency and costs without adding meaningful business value.
Audit your tracking code in every PR review; if it isn't documented in your schema, it doesn't exist.
Focus on Cohort Analysis over aggregate metrics to understand if your retention is actually improving over time.
content.next_steps: Once your tracking is live, spend 48 hours observing user behavior without making code changes. Then, perform a single, data-informed pivot on your most critical funnel bottleneck.
content.conversion_block: Stop building in the dark and start shipping products with data-backed confidence. The difference between a side project and a business is the ability to measure your success. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
